creator: barbara.xxx1975@libero.it
text:
Upgrading postgresql83-server, I've *accidentally* noted the following line in files/pkg-message-server.in that confused me for a couple of seconds.
Probably no one is searching the file there (and probably few are reading the message :) )
Note the path.

Please note that if you use the rc script,
%%PREFIX%%/etc/rc.conf/postgresql, to initialize the database, unicode
(UTF-8) will be used to store character data by default.

Fix: 

Fix the path to the rc script in files/pkg-message-server.in
How-To-Repeat: Install one of the postgresql server ports and read the pkg-message.
